rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF GORDON & REES LLP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (Part 1 of 2) Plaintiff¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication as to the second and third causes of action is granted as to Defendant Lopez. Summary adjudication as to the second and third causes of action is denied as to Defendant Koehler. Plaintiff has shifted the burden as to the second cause of action for account stated as to Defendant Lopez. Plaintiff has produced evidence of financial transactions arising from the parties¿ agreement for Plaintiff to provide legal services. (See Declaration of Zarzour in Support of Motion, ¶6, Exh. A, C.) Plaintiff also produces several emails that Defendants assented to the $110,158.53 amount owed, and promised to pay the amount. (See Declaration of Spector, Exh. A. Tr. 86:13-87:9, Exh. 8; Tr. 108:7-110:9, Exh. 24; Tr. 113:25-115:3; see also Gleason v. Klamer, 103 Cal. App. 3d 782, 786-87 (Ct. App. 1980).) The burden then shifts to Defendant Lopez to demonstrate that a triable issue of material fact exists. Defendant Lopez failed to produce admissible evidence showing that he did not assent to the amount owed on the invoice. The affirmative defenses raised by Plaintiff in its Opposition were not addressed by the Court as Defendants¿ Opposition exceeded the 20 page limit under CRC 3.113(d). Plaintiff has shifted the burden as to the third cause of action for open book account against Defendant Lopez. Plaintiff has produced evidence of the financial transactions between the parties, and the account of debits and credits involved in the transaction. (See Declaration of Zarzour, Exh. C.) As such, the burden shifts to Defendant Lopez. Defendant Lopez failed to produce any admissible evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact exists. (See Part 2 of 2). =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON & REES LLP VS. STEVEN ANTHONY LOPEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Gordon & Rees Llp Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 6, Plaintiff Gordon & Rees LLP'S Motion For Summary Adjudication (Part 2 of 2) Plaintiff shifted the burden as to the second and third causes of action against Defendant Koehler. Plaintiff has produced a fee waiver agreement, signed by Defendant Koehler, acknowledging its representation of Defendant Koehler. (Declaration of Zarzour, Exh. B.) Defendant also produces emails from Koehler offering to give a painting to Plaintiff to satisfy the debt. (Declaration of Spector, Lopez Deposition, Exhs. 8, 24, 27.) However, Defendant Koehler has produced evidence showing that a triable issue of material facts exists. Koehler shows that the invoices were addressed to Defendant Lopez, and that Plaintiff never directed, addressed, or named him as the debtor on the account. (Declaration of Koehler in Opposition to Plaintiff¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication, ¶20.) Defendant Koehler stated that he only assisted Defendant Lopez with reviewing the invoices and communications because he had an accounting background. (Id.) As such, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff Defendant Kohler is a debtor to the financial transactions for the second and third causes of action. Defendants¿ evidentiary objections are overruled. Plaintiff¿s evidentiary objections are sustained to the extent that the evidence lacks foundation, constitutes legal conclusions, constitutes expert opinion by lay witnesses, is not relevant, speculative, and conclusory. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of part 2 of 2) =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MO KUEN NG et al VS. YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication is granted as to the first and second causes of action and denied as to the third and fourth causes of action of the First Amended Complaint. Defendant¿s Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Pursuant to California Evidence Code section 452(d), the court takes judicial notice of the fact that a Complaint and First Amended Complaint were filed in this case. The court does not take judicial notice of the contents of the complaints. Plaintiff¿s Request for Judicial Notice is granted in part and denied in part. As to the Request for Judicial Notice no. 1, the United States Lifeguard Standards Coalition Review, the request is denied pursuant to CRC 3.1306(c), which requires the party requesting judicial notice to provide the court and each party with a copy of the material. As to the Request for Judicial Notice no. 2, the American Lifeguard Association internet webpage, the request is granted as to the fact that the webpage exists. The court will not take judicial notice of its contents. (Ragland v. U.S. Bank Nat. Assn. (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 182, 194). As to the Request for Judicial Notice no. 3, the Redwood Group Memorandum, the request is denied as it does not fall under any of the categories of judicially noticeable documents under Cal. Evidence Code sections 451 or 452. Plaintiff¿s Request for Continuance is denied. Pursuant to CCP §437(c)(h) a request for a continuance must be in the form of an affidavit. As to the first count of the first cause of action for premises liability negligence and the first count of the second cause of action for general negligence, the injury fell within the scope of a valid release. As to the second count of the first cause of action for willful failure to warn, no evidence was presented to satisfy the three prong test for willfulness (Manuel v. Pacific Gas & Elec. Co. (2009) 173 Cal. App. 4th 927, 945). As to the second count of the second cause of action for negligent hiring, retention, and supervision, no evidence was presented to support this cause of action. As to the third cause of action for loss of consortium, sufficient facts were plead under a gross negligence theory. As to the fourth cause of action for gross negligence, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether failure to put non-slip tape on a lifeguard chair amounts to an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of care. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GENERAL WORKING INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MOTI KAHANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Responses To Form Interoggs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiff GENERAL WORKING INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses to Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF GENERAL WORKINGS INC.¿S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN 30 DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO PLAINTIFF¿S FORM INTERROGATORIES-GENERAL SET ONE; (2) DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO PLAINTIFF¿S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE (3) DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA WILL PERMIT PLAINTIFF¿S COUNSEL TO INSPECT AND COPY THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED IN PLAINTIFF¿S INSPECTION DEMAND. COMPLIANCE WITH THIS REQUEST MUST OCCUR WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER AND ALL OBJECTIONS, EXCEPT ASSERTIONS OF ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE ARE DEEMED WAIVED. (4) DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA WILL PROVIDE WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER RESPONSES EITHER ADMITTING, DENYING, OR ASSERTING AN INABILITY TO ADMIT OR DENY EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION SERVED ON IT BY PLAINTIFF. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE RESPONSE IS SOMETHING OTHER THAN A UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA WILL PROVIDED THE INFORMATION REQUESTED BY FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA DISPUTES THAT IT RECEIVED THE ORIGINAL DISCOVERY REQUESTS IN THE MAIL AND ASSERTED BY PLAINTIFF, THERE IS NO DISPUTE THAT HE HAS HAD COPIES OF THOSE REQUESTS SINCE JANUARY, 2013 AND SETS FORTH NO LEGITIMATE REASONS FOR WHY RESPONSES WERE NOT PROVIDED BEFORE PLAINTIFF FILES HER MOTION. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANT MOTI KAHANA AND HIS ATTORNEY IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500 WITH PAYMENT TO BE MADE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF WILL PREPARE AND PROVIDE TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT AN ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING FOR APPROVAL AS TO FROM AND PRESENT THE ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING FOR SIGNATURE. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 2: Cross Complainant MICHAEL BEESON'S Motion to Compel Supplemental Responses to Special Interrogatories and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents - Set One And Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff JOHN STEVENS' Motion To Compel Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents - Set One And Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA HAVE FILED NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION AND FAILED TO FILE ANY OPPOSITION TO AN EARLIER MOTION SEEKING SIMILAR RELIEF WHICH WAS MOTION WAS GRANTED ON JULY 9, 2013. SINCE THE DATE OF JULY 9TH ORDER DEFENDANTS HAVE MADE NO ATTEMPT TO COMPLY. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL ENTER THE FOLLOWING ORDER: (1) WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT, JOHN STEVEN¿S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ¿ SET ONE, WHICH RESPONSE SHALL, AT A MINIMUM, IDENTIFY BY BATE STAMP EACH DOCUMENT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED WITH REFERENCE TO THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENT REQUEST. (2) WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA AND THEIR COUNSEL WILL PAY TO PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN STEVEN MONETARY SANCTIONS IS THE AMOUNT OF $1,327.50 PLUS THE MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2.290.00 PREVIOUSLY AWARDED BY THE COURT IN ITS ORDER OF July 9, 2013. (3) IF DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA FAIL TO FULLY COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF PARAGRAPHS (1) AND (2) WITHIN THE 30 DAYS SPECIFIED ABOVE, SAID DEFENDANTS WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM OFFERING ANY DOCUMENTS IN SUPPORT OF THEIR DEFENSES IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF¿S COMPLAINT AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA WILL BE DISMISSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN STEVENS WILL PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff JOHN STEVENS' Motion to Compel Further Responses to a Request for the Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA HAVE FILED NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION AND FAILED TO FILE ANY OPPOSITION TO AN EARLIER MOTION SEEKING SIMILAR RELIEF WHICH WAS MOTION WAS GRANTED ON JULY 9, 2013. SINCE THE DATE OF JULY 9TH ORDER DEFENDANTS HAVE MADE NO ATTEMPT TO COMPLY. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL ENTER THE FOLLOWING ORDER: (1) WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT, JOHN STEVEN¿S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ¿ SET ONE, WHICH RESPONSE SHALL, AT A MINIMUM, IDENTIFY BY BATE STAMP EACH DOCUMENT PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED WITH REFERENCE TO THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENT REQUEST. (2) WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA AND THEIR COUNSEL WILL PAY TO PLAINTIFF AND CROSS DEFENDANT JOHN STEVEN MONETARY SANCTIONS IS THE AMOUNT OF $1,327.50 PLUS THE MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2.290.00 PREVIOUSLY AWARDED BY THE COURT IN ITS ORDER OF July 9, 2013. (3) IF DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA FAIL TO FULLY COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF PARAGRAPHS (1) AND (2) WITHIN THE 30 DAYS SPECIFIED ABOVE, SAID DEFENDANTS WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM OFFERING ANY DOCUMENTS IN SUPPORT OF THEIR DEFENSES IN RESPONSE TO PLAINTIFF¿S COMPLAINT AND THE CROSS-COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA WILL BE DISMISSED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF AND CROSS-DEFENDANT JOHN STEVENS WILL PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS TOURAJ BOUSSINA AND PERRY BOUSSINA FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE HSIEH VS. SOLAR POWER INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff IRENE HSIEH'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses For Adverse Inference, Appointment Of Forensic Expert, And Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER: (1) DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF ALL DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1,2 & 5, WHICH REFER OR RELATE IN ANY MANNER TO SOLAR HUB UTILITIES OR THE SOLAR HUB TRANSACTION. (2) DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFF ALL DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NO. 1 & 2 WHICH REFER OR RELATE IN ANY MANNER TO CHINA DEVELOPMENT BANK, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, ANY DOCUMENTS REFLECTING ANY TRANSACTION OR POTENTIAL TRANSACTION BETWEEN CHINA DEVELOPMENT BANK AND SPI. (3) DEFENDANTS¿ OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NOS. 9, 11, 16, 19 ARE OVERRULED AND, TO THE EXTENT THAT IT HAS NOT ALREADY DONE SO, DEFENDANT WILL PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS. (4) TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT IS CLAIMING AN ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE OR A CLAIM OF THE RIGHT OF PRIVACY IN WITHHOLDING PRODUCTION OF ANY REQUESTED DOCUMENT IT WILL PROVIDE A LIST OF SUCH DOCUMENTS, THE CLAIMED BASIS FOR THE ASSERTION OF ANY SUCH PRIVILEGE AND DESCRIPTION OF THE DOCUMENT SUFFICIENT TO ENABLE PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. GIVEN ALL THE CIRCUMSTANCE REFLECTED IN THE PAPERS FILED BY BOTH PARTIES I AM NOT INCLINED TO IMPOSE ANY MONETARY SANCTIONS. ALTHOUGH THERE WAS EXTENSIVE COMMUNICATION BETWEEN THE PARTIES, PLAINTIFF AT NO TIME SOUGHT TO MAKE ITS REQUEST MORE SPECIFIC, PARTICULARLY AS TO REQUESTS NO 1 & 2 AND SHE REJECTED ANY SUGGESTIONS OFFERED BY DEFENDANT TO NARROW THE REQUESTS. ON THE OTHER HAND IT APPEARS THAT DEFENDANT IN RESPONDING TO THE REQUESTS DID A LITTLE CHERRY PICKING IN HOW IT WAS GOING TO RESPOND. (SEE EXHIBIT 8 TO THE DECLARATION OF GAUTAM DUTTA.) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE ORDER FOR SIGNING BY THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 5, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERIKA JOHNSON VS. MCCORMACK BARON SALAZAR INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT CONEYBEARE INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Coneybeare Inc.¿s demurrer to the entire First Amended Complaint and first cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend to plead facts, in good faith, that Darius was acting in the course and scope of his employment when the alleged incident occurred. (See CACI 3720.) To the extent that Plaintiff is alleging a cause of action for negligent hiring and retention, Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend to plead facts, in good faith, that Coneybeare knew or should have known that Darius was unfit to perform his job, or would create a harm, and that particular harm materialized. (See Phillips v. TLC Plumbing, Inc. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1133, 1139.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CYNTHIA WALKER VS. NEIMAN MARCUS HOLDINGS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbritration And To Dismiss Or In The Alternative To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, September 5, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbritration And To Dismiss Or In The Alternative To Stay Proceedings The motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Plaintiff fails to provide a declaration explaining how the contract was presented in a take it or leave it matter or that she did not receive the AAA rules. Even if the Court finds that those assertions are undisputed, plaintiff merely demonstrates procedural unconscionability. Defendant¿s ability to modify the contract in a unilateral fashion is not substantively unconscionable under In re Halliburton, (Tex. 2002) 80 S.W.3d 566 and Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc., (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695, 708, fn. 7. The Texas choice of law and Texas arbitrator provision is severable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
